_description: Reference: dynamic map block (W5.2) — multi-provider geolocation map. Default provider is Leaflet+OSM (no key required, works for every tenant out of the box). Mapbox + Google are opt-in alternatives that require per-tenant keys configured in `content_settings.map_providers` (Fernet-encrypted at rest in the new W3.3 column). Multi-provider soft-fall-back at the SERVER boundary: when a tenant requests `provider: 'mapbox'` but their `content_settings.map_providers.mapbox` token is missing, the renderer downgrades to Leaflet+OSM tiles and emits a `provider_fallback` log instead of black-screening.
_provider_decision: Per §13.3=B LOCKED: Leaflet default · Mapbox opt-in · Google opt-in. Never bundle Mapbox or Google client libraries in the core renderer — they load lazily on first map block render only when the tenant has the matching key.

_provider_options.leaflet: Default. OpenStreetMap tiles. No key. Works on every tenant.
_provider_options.mapbox: Requires content_settings.map_providers.mapbox token. Falls back to leaflet on missing key.
_provider_options.google: Requires content_settings.map_providers.google_maps + google_signing_secret (Maps Static needs HMAC signed requests). Falls back to leaflet on missing key.

_lat_lng_bearing_sources: idap.businesses (lat / lng columns from Google Maps scrape)
idap.streets (street-level lat/lng — only loads top-200 to avoid client overload)
Any custom `content_data_sources` row exposing lat + lng fields
_anti_patterns: Do NOT include `_googleMapsKey` or `_mapboxToken` or any `_*`-prefixed prop in your authored payload — the W3.3 strip-on-write helper wipes them on every write. The renderer injects per-tenant keys server-side at render time only.
If a tenant's data source has no lat/lng fields, the map block 422s at validation time. Use `template_get_help → marketplace.dynamic_map` to see the per-source field requirements before binding.
Sparkline-density coordinate clusters (>500 markers in one viewport) hit the SQL LIMIT 500 cap; use the `filter` block to narrow before increasing limit.
